evidence: Video-travel readers still compose values through multi-source fallback ladders: `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts` (`buildSegmentState`, lines 300-397) repeatedly resolves from `params` -> `taskViewContract`/`orig` -> `orchestratorDetails` with `??` chains.

Task completion/billing still preserve explicit legacy lookup paths instead of a single canonical reference contract: `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts` lines 391-463 and `supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts` lines 296-313 both track `'canonical' | 'legacy'` and warn on legacy fallback hits.

Lightbox orchestration still relies on broad prop/model composition with pass-through mapping layers: `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` lines 417-460 and `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx` lines 686-730 build large workflow prop objects, then `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/layouts/LightboxLayout.tsx` consumes nested bags and spread props (`workflowBarProps`, `buttonGroupProps`).

A deprecated Supabase abstraction remains exported as first-class surface in `src/integrations/supabase/legacy/index.ts` line 11 while active hooks (`src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts`) already use canonical `getSupabaseClient`, leaving dual entrypoints alive.

Server completion flow is partially standardized: `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts` normalizes failures into `CompletionError` with `code/context/recoverable/metadata`, but `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts` still throws ad-hoc `Error` strings for fetch/update/persistence failures.

Shot editor callback adapters in `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts` use mixed failure contracts for neighboring actions: delete path throws via `handleAndRethrow`, add-to-shot paths return `false`, and create-shot path returns `undefined` on failure.
error_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Error contracts are still not uniform across completion/billing and shot-editor interaction boundaries, so callers cannot reliably branch on one stable failure shape.

findings.summary: Video-travel flow still depends on contract-plus-legacy fallback ladders instead of one canonical read interface.
evidence: `buildSegmentState` in `individualTravelSegment.ts` (lines 300-397) repeatedly merges `params`, `orig`, `taskViewContract`, and `orchestratorDetails` field-by-field (`motion_mode`, prompts, model, guidance, etc.).
`VideoTravelDetails.tsx` still documents and implements legacy fallback behavior for structure video fields (lines 157-174).
`orchestrator.ts` and `calculate-task-cost/index.ts` retain explicit legacy lookup source tracking (`'canonical' | 'legacy'`) and warning paths when legacy references match.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single normalized travel payload adapter (UI + edge shared contract) that materializes canonical fields once; make all consumers read only normalized fields and gate legacy read paths behind one migration adapter module with telemetry.

findings.summary: Deprecated Supabase facade remains an active exported abstraction, preserving dual client surfaces.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/legacy/index.ts` line 11 still exports `supabase` and `getLegacySupabaseClient` as public compatibility API.
Current hook code in `useTasks.ts` and `useGenerationMutations.ts` imports canonical `getSupabaseClient`, showing migration is underway but dual surface remains available for drift.
findings.suggestion: Demote legacy facade to internal-only migration adapter (non-public path), enforce no-new-import policy via CI, and switch remaining allowlisted importers to canonical client before removing the legacy index export.

findings.summary: Lightbox orchestration still incurs heavy prop-bag assembly/tunneling across render-model and layout layers.
evidence: Both render-model hooks construct large `useLightboxWorkflowProps` payloads (Image: lines 686-730, Video: lines 417-460), including nested `shotWorkflow`, `actions`, and `buttonGroupProps` bags.
`LightboxLayout.tsx` then pulls nested props and re-maps callbacks/fields into child components (`workflowBarProps`, button group spreads, segment slot mapping), increasing indirection.
`MediaGallery/index.tsx` remains a broad orchestration point with large prop unpacking and many controller hook joins (lines 68-220), which feeds the same wide lightbox surface.
findings.suggestion: Define a narrowed lightbox controller interface (command/query objects) per mode (image/video) and pass mode-specific controllers to layout/components instead of forwarding broad nested prop structures.

findings.summary: ShotImageManager container remains a multi-manager aggregation hub, creating a high-indirection controller abstraction.
evidence: `ShotImageManagerContainer.tsx` defines a large aggregate state shape (`ShotImageManagerContainerState`, lines 48-69) bundling many hook return types and manager groups.

`useShotImageManagerContainerState` wires many manager hooks (`useOptimisticOrder`, `useExternalGenerations`, `useSelection`, `useLightbox`, `useDragAndDrop`, `useBatchOperations`, `useMobileGestures`) and mediates cross-manager concerns in one place (lines 136-230).
Similar orchestration concentration appears in `MediaGallery/index.tsx`, indicating repeated controller-aggregation pattern instead of smaller feature-boundary controllers.
findings.suggestion: Split container orchestration by bounded contexts (selection/order, segment ops, lightbox/nav) with explicit facades and compose them at render boundary to reduce cross-manager coupling.

findings.summary: Orchestrator completion still emits stringified `Error` failures while sibling completion code uses structured error envelopes.
evidence: `orchestrator.ts` throws raw `Error` with interpolated strings in multiple paths (e.g., orchestrator fetch failure, failed status update, billing metadata persistence fallback failure).
`generation.ts` catches and rethrows normalized `CompletionError` via `toCompletionError(...)`, preserving `error_code`, `recoverable`, `context`, and `metadata` for downstream policy/logging.
findings.suggestion: Adopt the same structured completion error type in `orchestrator.ts` (or a shared edge error wrapper) and include stable fields (`code`, `context`, `recoverable`, `metadata`) for all thrown failures, especially billing persistence/update branches.

findings.summary: Shot editor callback adapters still mix throw/sentinel failure semantics across comparable operations.
evidence: `handleDeleteSegment` uses `handleAndRethrow(...)` and propagates exceptions for failure.
`handleAddToShotAdapter` and `handleAddToShotWithoutPositionAdapter` catch and return `false`, while `handleCreateShotAdapter` catches and returns `undefined`.
These are peer user actions in one adapter surface, but they expose different caller contracts for failure detection.
findings.suggestion: Standardize all adapter callbacks on one explicit result contract (for example `OperationResult<T>`), and make callers consume that contract instead of mixing exception flow with boolean/undefined sentinels.
